---
title: "Wastewater Surveillance Data Provenance: Impact of Undocumented Preprocessing"
date: today
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
jupyter: julia-1.10
---

## intro

This document demonstrates the quantitative impact of inadequate methods paradata documentation in wastewater-based epidemiology. We examine a scenario where preprocessing steps remain undocumented, leading to systematic misinterpretation of epidemic dynamics.

## Setup
```{julia}
using DataFrames
using CSV
using CairoMakie
using Statistics
using Random
using DSP

Random.seed!(42)
mkpath("phes_odm_outputs")
```

## PHES-ODM Schema Implementation

### Program Description Tables
```{julia}
sites = DataFrame(
    siteID = ["WWTP_001"],
    siteName = ["Municipal WWTP #1"],
    siteType = ["wastewater_treatment_plant"],
    geoLat = [45.5017],
    geoLong = [-73.5673],
    description = ["Primary wastewater treatment plant serving downtown sewershed"],
    polygonID = ["POLY_001"]
)

polygons = DataFrame(
    polygonID = ["POLY_001"],
    polygonName = ["Downtown Sewershed"],
    polygonType = ["sewershed"],
    populationServed = [50000]
)

organizations = DataFrame(
    organizationID = ["ORG_001", "ORG_002"],
    organizationName = ["Public Health Lab", "Municipal Water Utility"],
    organizationType = ["laboratory", "utility"]
)

contacts = DataFrame(
    contactID = ["CONT_001", "CONT_002"],
    contactName = ["Lab Director", "Field Technician"],
    organizationID = ["ORG_001", "ORG_002"],
    email = ["director@phlab.gov", "tech@utility.gov"]
)

datasets = DataFrame(
    datasetID = ["DS_001"],
    datasetName = ["Municipal WBE Program 2024"],
    custodianContactID = ["CONT_001"],
    funderOrganizationID = ["ORG_001"],
    license = ["CC-BY-4.0"],
    purpose = ["public_health_surveillance"]
)

protocols = DataFrame(
    protocolID = ["PROT_001", "PROT_002", "PROT_003", "PROT_004"],
    protocolName = ["Composite Sampling SOP", "RNA Extraction Protocol", 
                    "RT-qPCR N1 Gene Assay", "7-day Moving Average Smoothing"],
    protocolType = ["sampling", "extraction", "measurement", "data_transformation"],
    protocolVersion = ["1.2", "2.0", "1.5", "1.0"],
    organizationID = ["ORG_002", "ORG_001", "ORG_001", "ORG_001"]
)

println("Created $(nrow(sites)) site(s)")
println("Created $(nrow(protocols)) protocol(s)")
```

### Sample Reports
```{julia}
n_days = 120
start_date = Date(2024, 1, 1)

samples = DataFrame(
    sampleID = ["SAMPLE_" * lpad(i, 4, '0') for i in 1:n_days],
    sampleType = fill("wastewater_composite", n_days),
    siteID = fill("WWTP_001", n_days),
    collectionDate = [start_date + Day(i-1) for i in 1:n_days],
    collectionStartTime = [DateTime(start_date + Day(i-1), Time(6, 0)) for i in 1:n_days],
    collectionEndTime = [DateTime(start_date + Day(i), Time(6, 0)) for i in 1:n_days],
    collectionMethodID = fill("PROT_001", n_days),
    specimenType = fill("wastewater", n_days),
    reportable = fill(true, n_days),
    datasetID = fill("DS_001", n_days),
    contactID = fill("CONT_002", n_days)
)

println("Generated $(nrow(samples)) samples")
```

## Synthetic Epidemic Curve

Generate realistic SARS-CoV-2 viral load data using a Gaussian epidemic curve.
```{julia}
peak_day = 40
amplitude = 1500
width = 15

days = 1:n_days
base_signal = amplitude .* exp.(-((days .- peak_day).^2) ./ (2 * width^2))

noise = randn(n_days) .* 200

outlier_days = rand(1:n_days, 5)
outliers = zeros(n_days)
outliers[outlier_days] .= rand([-1000, -500, 500, 1000], 5)

raw_viral_load = max.(0, base_signal .+ noise .+ outliers)

println("Peak day: $peak_day")
println("Max viral load: $(round(maximum(raw_viral_load), digits=1)) gc/L")
println("Mean viral load: $(round(mean(raw_viral_load), digits=1)) gc/L")
```
```{julia}
fig = Figure(resolution=(1000, 400))
ax = Axis(fig[1,1], 
    xlabel="Day", 
    ylabel="Viral Load (gc/L)",
    title="Raw SARS-CoV-2 Viral Load Time Series")
lines!(ax, days, raw_viral_load, color=:blue, linewidth=2, label="Raw Data")
axislegend(ax, position=:rt)
fig
```

## Raw Measure Reports
```{julia}
measures_raw = DataFrame(
    measureID = ["MEAS_RAW_" * lpad(i, 4, '0') for i in 1:n_days],
    sampleID = samples.sampleID,
    siteID = fill("WWTP_001", n_days),
    measureDate = samples.collectionDate,
    measure = fill("sarscov2_n1_gene", n_days),
    aggregation = fill("single", n_days),
    value = raw_viral_load,
    unit = fill("gc_per_l", n_days),
    specimen = fill("wastewater", n_days),
    fraction = fill("solid", n_days),
    methodID = fill("PROT_003", n_days),
    qualityFlag = fill("no_quality_concerns", n_days),
    reportable = fill(true, n_days),
    datasetID = fill("DS_001", n_days),
    contactID = fill("CONT_001", n_days)
)

println("Created $(nrow(measures_raw)) raw measurements")
```

## Data Preprocessing

Apply 7-day moving average filter.
```{julia}
window_size = 7
filtered_viral_load = DSP.filt(ones(window_size) / window_size, [1.0], raw_viral_load)

println("Filtered mean: $(round(mean(filtered_viral_load), digits=1)) gc/L")
println("Filtered std: $(round(std(filtered_viral_load), digits=1)) gc/L")
```

## Paradata Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Undocumented Filtering

Filtered data stored with unchanged methodID and aggregation fields.
```{julia}
measures_filtered_undocumented = DataFrame(
    measureID = ["MEAS_FILT_" * lpad(i, 4, '0') for i in 1:n_days],
    sampleID = samples.sampleID,
    siteID = fill("WWTP_001", n_days),
    measureDate = samples.collectionDate,
    measure = fill("sarscov2_n1_gene", n_days),
    aggregation = fill("single", n_days),
    value = filtered_viral_load,
    unit = fill("gc_per_l", n_days),
    specimen = fill("wastewater", n_days),
    fraction = fill("solid", n_days),
    methodID = fill("PROT_003", n_days),
    qualityFlag = fill("no_quality_concerns", n_days),
    reportable = fill(true, n_days),
    datasetID = fill("DS_001", n_days),
    contactID = fill("CONT_001", n_days)
)

println("Created undocumented filtered data")
```

### Scenario 2: Documented Filtering

Proper paradata documentation with updated fields and quality reports.
```{julia}
measures_filtered_documented = DataFrame(
    measureID = ["MEAS_FILT_DOC_" * lpad(i, 4, '0') for i in 1:n_days],
    sampleID = samples.sampleID,
    siteID = fill("WWTP_001", n_days),
    measureDate = samples.collectionDate,
    measure = fill("sarscov2_n1_gene", n_days),
    aggregation = fill("7_day_moving_average", n_days),
    value = filtered_viral_load,
    unit = fill("gc_per_l", n_days),
    specimen = fill("wastewater", n_days),
    fraction = fill("solid", n_days),
    methodID = fill("PROT_004", n_days),
    qualityFlag = fill("no_quality_concerns", n_days),
    reportable = fill(true, n_days),
    datasetID = fill("DS_001", n_days),
    contactID = fill("CONT_001", n_days)
)

quality_reports = DataFrame(
    qualityFlagID = ["QF_" * lpad(i, 4, '0') for i in 1:n_days],
    measureID = measures_filtered_documented.measureID,
    qualityFlag = fill("data_transformed", n_days),
    notes = fill("Applied 7-day moving average filter per PROT_004", n_days),
    contactID = fill("CONT_001", n_days)
)

println("Created documented filtered data with quality reports")
```

## Analysis
```{julia}
function calculate_stats(values, label)
    return DataFrame(
        Metric = label,
        Mean = mean(values),
        StdDev = std(values),
        CV = std(values) / mean(values),
        Max = maximum(values),
        GrowthRate = mean(diff(values))
    )
end

raw_stats = calculate_stats(raw_viral_load, "Raw")
filtered_stats = calculate_stats(filtered_viral_load, "Filtered")

comparison = vcat(raw_stats, filtered_stats)

diff_pct = DataFrame(
    Metric = ["Mean", "Std Dev", "CV", "Max", "Growth Rate"],
    Raw = [raw_stats[1, :Mean], raw_stats[1, :StdDev], raw_stats[1, :CV], 
           raw_stats[1, :Max], raw_stats[1, :GrowthRate]],
    Filtered = [filtered_stats[1, :Mean], filtered_stats[1, :StdDev], 
                filtered_stats[1, :CV], filtered_stats[1, :Max], 
                filtered_stats[1, :GrowthRate]]
)

diff_pct[!, :Difference_Pct] = ((diff_pct.Filtered .- diff_pct.Raw) ./ diff_pct.Raw) .* 100

println("Statistical Comparison:")
println(diff_pct)
```

## Visualization
```{julia}
fig = Figure(resolution=(1400, 1000))

ax1 = Axis(fig[1, :], 
    xlabel="Day", 
    ylabel="Viral Load (gene copies/L)",
    title="Impact of Undocumented Data Preprocessing on Wastewater Surveillance")

lines!(ax1, days, raw_viral_load, 
    color=:blue, linewidth=2, label="Raw Data")
lines!(ax1, days, filtered_viral_load, 
    color=:orange, linewidth=2, linestyle=:dash, 
    label="Filtered (Undocumented methodID)")
lines!(ax1, days, filtered_viral_load, 
    color=:green, linewidth=2, 
    label="Filtered (Documented methodID)")

axislegend(ax1, position=:rt)

ax2 = Axis(fig[2, 1],
    xlabel="",
    ylabel="Std Dev (gc/L)",
    title="Standard Deviation Impact",
    xticks=([1, 2], ["Raw", "Filtered"]))

barplot!(ax2, [1, 2], [raw_stats[1, :StdDev], filtered_stats[1, :StdDev]],
    color=[:steelblue, :coral])

text!(ax2, 1.5, filtered_stats[1, :StdDev] * 0.5,
    text="$(round(diff_pct[2, :Difference_Pct], digits=1))%",
    color=:red, fontsize=20, align=(:center, :center))

ax3 = Axis(fig[2, 2],
    xlabel="",
    ylabel="Max (gc/L)",
    title="Maximum Value Impact",
    xticks=([1, 2], ["Raw", "Filtered"]))

barplot!(ax3, [1, 2], [raw_stats[1, :Max], filtered_stats[1, :Max]],
    color=[:steelblue, :coral])

text!(ax3, 1.5, filtered_stats[1, :Max] * 0.5,
    text="$(round(diff_pct[4, :Difference_Pct], digits=1))%",
    color=:red, fontsize=20, align=(:center, :center))

save("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_provenance_comparison.png", fig, px_per_unit=2)
fig
```

## Export PHES-ODM Tables
```{julia}
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_sites.csv", sites)
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_polygons.csv", polygons)
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_organizations.csv", organizations)
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_contacts.csv", contacts)
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_datasets.csv", datasets)
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_protocols.csv", protocols)
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_samples.csv", samples)
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_measures_raw.csv", measures_raw)
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_measures_filtered_undocumented.csv", measures_filtered_undocumented)
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_measures_filtered_documented.csv", measures_filtered_documented)
CSV.write("phes_odm_outputs/phes_odm_quality_reports.csv", quality_reports)

println("Exported 11 PHES-ODM compliant CSV files")
```


The analysis demonstrates substantial quantitative impacts of undocumented preprocessing:

- Standard deviation reduced by $(round(abs(diff_pct[2, :Difference_Pct]), digits=1))%
- Maximum value reduced by $(round(abs(diff_pct[4, :Difference_Pct]), digits=1))%  
- Growth rate differs by $(round(abs(diff_pct[5, :Difference_Pct]), digits=1))%

Without proper paradata documentation, filtered data is indistinguishable from raw data within the PHES-ODM schema, leading to miscalibrated outbreak detection algorithms, biased forecasting models, and invalid cross-site comparisons.
